steps.title: Identify intercepts and slope of the first equation
steps.content:
For $x + y = 53$:
  • $y$-intercept: set $x = 0 \Rightarrow y = 53$, giving $(0, 53)$
  • $x$-intercept: set $y = 0 \Rightarrow x = 53$, giving $(53, 0)$
  • Slope: $-1$ (line slants downward)

steps.title: Identify intercepts and slope of the second equation
steps.content:
For $11x + 18y = 730$:
  • $y$-intercept: set $x = 0 \Rightarrow y = \frac{730}{18} \approx 41$, giving $(0, 41)$
  • $x$-intercept: set $y = 0 \Rightarrow x = \frac{730}{11} \approx 66$, giving $(66, 0)$
  • Slope: $-\frac{11}{18}$ (line slants downward)

steps.title: Select the graph matching both equations
steps.content:
The correct graph shows both lines slanting downward: the first through $(0, 53)$ and $(53, 0)$, the second through $(0, 41)$ and $(66, 0)$.

Testing choices:
  • Choice A: Second line slants upward — incorrect slope
  • Choice B: Both lines slant downward; intercepts match
  • Choice C: Second line slants upward — incorrect slope
  • Choice D: First line is horizontal — incorrect slope
That matches choice B — B is correct.
